briefdescription.para: 24-bit RGB format with 8 bits per component.

detaileddescription.para: Below shows how data are organized in memory.
Byte0Byte1Byte2 7......015.....823....16 |BbbbbbbbGgggggggRrrrrrrr|... 24-bit RGB

briefdescription.para: 1-bit monochrome format with 1 bit per pixel, thus each byte represent 8 pixels Two variants, with black being either represented by 0 or 1

detaileddescription.para: Below shows how data are organized in memory.
Byte0|Byte1| 7......07......0 |MMMMMMMM|MMMMMMMM|... Monochrome (0=Black 1=White)

briefdescription.para: Monochrome (1=Black 0=White)
32-bit RGB format with 8 bits per component and 8 bits for alpha.

detaileddescription.para: Below shows how data are organized in memory.
Byte0Byte1Byte2Byte3 7......015.....823....1631....24 |BbbbbbbbGgggggggRrrrrrrrAaaaaaaa|... 32-bit ARGB

briefdescription.para: 16-bit RGB format packed into two bytes: 5 red bits [15:11], 6 green bits [10:5], 5 blue bits [4:0].

detaileddescription.para: Below shows how data are organized in memory.

briefdescription.para: 16-bit RGB format packed into two bytes.

detaileddescription.para: Byte swapped version of the PIXEL_FORMAT_RGB_565 format.

briefdescription.para: 8-bit Greyscale format

detaileddescription.para: Below shows how data are organized in memory.
Byte0|Byte1| 7......07......0 |Gggggggg|Gggggggg|... 8-bit Grayscale/Luminance, equivalent to GRAY, GREY, GRAY8, Y8, R8, etc...

briefdescription.para: 16-bit Greyscale format with 8-bit luminance and 8-bit for alpha

detaileddescription.para: Below shows how data are organized in memory.
Byte0Byte1| 7......015.....8 |GgggggggAaaaaaaa|... 8-bit Grayscale/Luminance with alpha

briefdescription.para: 32-bit RGB format with 8 bits per component and 8 bits unused.

detaileddescription.para: Below shows how data are organized in memory.
Byte0Byte1Byte2Byte3 7......015.....823....1631....24 |BbbbbbbbGgggggggRrrrrrrrXxxxxxxx|... 32-bit XRGB

briefdescription.para: 24-bit BGR format with 8 bits per component.

detaileddescription.para: Below shows how data are organized in memory.
Byte0Byte1Byte2 7......015.....823....16 |RrrrrrrrGgggggggBbbbbbbb|... 24-bit BGR

briefdescription.para: 32-bit BGR format with 8 bits per component and 8 bits for alpha.

detaileddescription.para: Below shows how data are organized in memory.
Byte0Byte1Byte2Byte3 7......015.....823....1631....24 |RrrrrrrrGgggggggBbbbbbbbAaaaaaaa|... 32-bit ABGR

briefdescription.para: 32-bit RGB format with 8 bits per component and 8 bits for alpha.

detaileddescription.para: Below shows how data are organized in memory.
Byte0Byte1Byte2Byte3 7......015.....823....1631....24 |AaaaaaaaBbbbbbbbGgggggggRrrrrrrr|... 32-bit RGBA

briefdescription.para: 32-bit BGR format with 8 bits per component and 8 bits for alpha.

detaileddescription.para: Below shows how data are organized in memory.
Byte0Byte1Byte2Byte3 7......015.....823....1631....24 |AaaaaaaaRrrrrrrrGgggggggBbbbbbbb|... 32-bit BGRA

briefdescription.para: This and higher values are display specific.

detaileddescription.para: Refer to the display header file.

briefdescription.para: Display pixel formats.

detaileddescription.para: Display pixel format enumeration.
In case a pixel format consists out of multiple bytes the byte order is big endian.

briefdescription.para: If selected, one octet represents 8 pixels ordered vertically, otherwise ordered horizontally.
If selected, the MSB represents the first pixel, otherwise MSB represents the last pixel.
Electrophoretic Display.
Screen has two alternating ram buffers.
Screen has x alignment constrained to width.
Display screen information.
No rotation.
Rotated 90 degrees clockwise.
Rotated 180 degrees clockwise.
Rotated 270 degrees clockwise.
Enumeration with possible display orientation.
Fired when controller reaches a configured scanline.
Fired at vertical sync / start of new frame.
Fired when a frame transfer to the panel or frame buffer update completes.
Display event types.
Let the driver execute its default handling.
The callback handled the event and the driver should skip its default processing for that event.
Display event callback return flags.

memberdef.argsstring: )(const struct device *dev, uint32_t evt, const struct display_event_data *data, void *user_data)
memberdef.name: display_event_cb_t

briefdescription.para: Called either in ISR context (if arg in_isr=true at register time, see display_register_event_cb ) or in thread context (if in_isr=false, driver will schedule work to call it).

detaileddescription.para: When called from ISR context the callback must be extremely fast and must not call blocking APIs or sleep.

parameterdescription.para: 'enum display_event' bit of event to handle
User data passed by driver to callback

simplesect.para: An 'enum display_event_result' flag, see its description for details.

memberdef.argsstring: (const struct device *dev, const uint16_t x, const uint16_t y, const struct display_buffer_descriptor *desc, const void *buf)
memberdef.name: display_write

briefdescription.para: Write data to display.

parameterdescription.para: x Coordinate of the upper left corner where to write the buffer
y Coordinate of the upper left corner where to write the buffer
Pointer to a structure describing the buffer layout

simplesect.para: 0 on success else negative errno code.

memberdef.argsstring: (const struct device *dev, const uint16_t x, const uint16_t y, const struct display_buffer_descriptor *desc, void *buf)
memberdef.name: display_read

briefdescription.para: Read data from display.

parameterdescription.para: x Coordinate of the upper left corner where to read from
y Coordinate of the upper left corner where to read from
Pointer to a structure describing the buffer layout

simplesect.para: 0 on success else negative errno code.

briefdescription.para: Clear the screen of the display device.

simplesect.para: 0 on success else negative errno code.

briefdescription.para: Get pointer to framebuffer for direct access.

simplesect.para: Pointer to frame buffer or NULL if direct framebuffer access is not supported

briefdescription.para: Turn display blanking on.

detaileddescription.para: This function blanks the complete display. The content of the frame buffer will be retained while blanking is enabled and the frame buffer will be accessible for read and write operations.
In case backlight control is supported by the driver the backlight is turned off. The backlight configuration is retained and accessible for configuration.
In case the driver supports display blanking the initial state of the driver would be the same as if this function was called.

simplesect.para: 0 on success else negative errno code.

briefdescription.para: Turn display blanking off.

detaileddescription.para: Restore the frame buffer content to the display. In case backlight control is supported by the driver the backlight configuration is restored.

simplesect.para: 0 on success else negative errno code.

briefdescription.para: Set the brightness of the display.

detaileddescription.para: Set the brightness of the display in steps of 1/256, where 255 is full brightness and 0 is minimal.

simplesect.para: 0 on success else negative errno code.

briefdescription.para: Set the contrast of the display.

detaileddescription.para: Set the contrast of the display in steps of 1/256, where 255 is maximum difference and 0 is minimal.

simplesect.para: 0 on success else negative errno code.

briefdescription.para: Get display capabilities.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to capabilities structure to populate

memberdef.argsstring: (const struct device *dev, const enum display_pixel_format pixel_format)
memberdef.name: display_set_pixel_format

briefdescription.para: Set pixel format used by the display.

parameterdescription.para: Pixel format to be used by display

simplesect.para: 0 on success else negative errno code.

memberdef.argsstring: (const struct device *dev, const enum display_orientation orientation)
memberdef.name: display_set_orientation

briefdescription.para: Set display orientation.

parameterdescription.para: Orientation to be used by display

simplesect.para: 0 on success else negative errno code.

memberdef.argsstring: (const struct device *dev, display_event_cb_t cb, void *user_data, uint32_t event_mask, bool in_isr, uint32_t *out_reg_handle)
memberdef.name: display_register_event_cb

briefdescription.para: Register event callback for a display device.

parameterdescription.para: User callback function pointer, see display_event_cb_t description
User data to be passed by driver to event callback
Mask of 'enum display_event' events upon which the callback will be called

If true, callback will be invoked in ISR context (shall be fast, non-blocking). If false, callback will be invoked in thread/workqueue context. A driver is allowed to implement just one of the invocation contexts.
Lets the caller prove ownership of the registration, and allows for safe unregistration

simplesect.para: Thread-context delivery should be preferred for non-critical work; keep ISR fast and optionally only set flags and schedule work, avoid scheduler APIs, mutexes, prints.
0 and a non-zero out_reg_handle value on success, otherwise a negative errno code.

parameterdescription.para: One of the events is not supported, or the requested invocation context is not supported.

briefdescription.para: Unregister event callback for a display device.

parameterdescription.para: Handle used to register the callback.

simplesect.para: 0 on success, otherwise a negative errno code.

parameterdescription.para: Not the owner, or already unregistered.
If it, or register_event_cb, is not implemented.

memberdef.name: DISPLAY_BITS_PER_PIXEL
memberdef.initializer: ((((fmt & PIXEL_FORMAT_RGB_888) >> 0) * 24U) + \ (((fmt & PIXEL_FORMAT_MONO01) >> 1) * 1U) + \ (((fmt & PIXEL_FORMAT_MONO10) >> 2) * 1U) + \ (((fmt & PIXEL_FORMAT_ARGB_8888) >> 3) * 32U) + \ (((fmt & PIXEL_FORMAT_RGB_565) >> 4) * 16U) + \ (((fmt & PIXEL_FORMAT_RGB_565X) >> 5) * 16U) + \ (((fmt & PIXEL_FORMAT_L_8) >> 6) * 8U) + \ (((fmt & PIXEL_FORMAT_AL_88) >> 7) * 16U) + \ (((fmt & PIXEL_FORMAT_XRGB_8888) >> 8) * 32U) + \ (((fmt & PIXEL_FORMAT_BGR_888) >> 9) * 24U) + \ (((fmt & PIXEL_FORMAT_ABGR_8888) >> 10) * 32U) + \ (((fmt & PIXEL_FORMAT_RGBA_8888) >> 11) * 32U) + \ (((fmt & PIXEL_FORMAT_BGRA_8888) >> 12) * 32U))

briefdescription.para: Bits required per pixel for display format.

detaileddescription.para: This macro expands to the number of bits required for a given display format. It can be used to allocate a framebuffer based on a given display format type. This does not work with any private pixel formats.

briefdescription.para: Interfaces for display controllers.
